item.title: How to Beat Your First Run in Everything Is Crab
item.description:
Pressure 1 is the first difficulty in Everything Is Crab , so it is where players will begin their journey. Right after completing the short tutorial, they will be on their own, so it's completely normal to fail a few times before getting the hang of it.

During a run, players will have to survive for a total of 20 minutes, and during this time, they will face dozens of small enemies, many Alpha hostiles, and four large bosses. This guide will provide some beginner tips and tricks for Everything Is Crab to ensure players that they are able to beat all bosses in Pressure 1 and make it out of the first stage in the game.

Follow the Question Marks

As players explore the map in Everything Is Crab , they will often find question marks at the edge of their screen. They should follow the question marks, as they will take gamers to a point of interest (POI). There, they can interact with a structure and pick a bonus or initiate an event.

After finding seven POIs, gamers will unlock the Pioneer Genetics, which increases the point of interest spawn rate by 50 percent. This will greatly increase players' stats throughout the run, as they will be able to claim more POI boosts. It pairs well with the Nomad evolution, as they can easily farm research points and level up faster.

Use Mutagen Points

Players will receive Mutagen Points by defeating tougher enemies in Everything Is Crab . With these points, they can reroll evolutions when they level up (1 Mutagen Point required) or increase the rarity of the selection of evolutions available to them upon level up (10 Mutagen Points required for the first rarity increase).

Choose Affinities and Evolution Wisely

As players level up and select evolutions, they will create affinities with certain types of species. There are five affinity types in Everything Is Crab : Predator, Prey, Gregarious, Imposing, and Trickster.

While players will end up picking evolutions associated with more than one affinity, it is often best to choose evolutions that go hand in hand with the affinities currently benefiting their strategy. For example, if they've chosen the Herbivore or Vegan evolution, picking Predator affinities won't have as much effect, as they won't get as much from meat as they do from fruits.

After leveling up a few times, players might want to pick what type of creature they want to be so they can focus on which affinities they will invest in.

Beware of Your Biome Before a Boss Fight Begins

Players will find it much more difficult to successfully defeat a boss if they're fighting in terrain or weather that is not ideal for their current evolution choices.

Subscribe to our newsletter for Everything Is Crab tips

Join our newsletter to get focused guides and practical strategies for Everything Is Crab — concise breakdowns of POIs, Mutagen point usage, affinity choices, and biome tactics so you can make smarter evolution decisions during your runs.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

To have an easier time fighting a boss, players will want to move to the grassy biome, as it has moderate weather and the terrain that is ideal for any creature. This way, they can focus on fighting the boss rather than having to find shade or constantly waiting for their dash or sprint cooldown to end.

Everything is Crab

Follow
Followed
Action
Roguelite
Strategy
Systems
Released
May 8, 2026
Developer(s)
Odd Dreams Digital
Publisher(s)
Secret Mode
Number of Players
Single-player
Steam Deck Compatibility
Unknown
8 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

PC Release Date
May 8, 2026
Genre(s)
Action, Roguelite, Strategy
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Pressure 1 is the first difficulty in Everything Is Crab , so it is where players will begin their journey. Right after completing the short tutorial, they will be on their own, so it's completely normal to fail a few times before getting the hang of it.

During a run, players will have to survive for a total of 20 minutes, and during this time, they will face dozens of small enemies, many Alpha hostiles, and four large bosses. This guide will provide some beginner tips and tricks for Everything Is Crab to ensure players that they are able to beat all bosses in Pressure 1 and make it out of the first stage in the game.

Follow the Question Marks

As players explore the map in Everything Is Crab , they will often find question marks at the edge of their screen. They should follow the question marks, as they will take gamers to a point of interest (POI). There, they can interact with a structure and pick a bonus or initiate an event.

After finding seven POIs, gamers will unlock the Pioneer Genetics, which increases the point of interest spawn rate by 50 percent. This will greatly increase players' stats throughout the run, as they will be able to claim more POI boosts. It pairs well with the Nomad evolution, as they can easily farm research points and level up faster.

Use Mutagen Points

Players will receive Mutagen Points by defeating tougher enemies in Everything Is Crab . With these points, they can reroll evolutions when they level up (1 Mutagen Point required) or increase the rarity of the selection of evolutions available to them upon level up (10 Mutagen Points required for the first rarity increase).

Choose Affinities and Evolution Wisely

As players level up and select evolutions, they will create affinities with certain types of species. There are five affinity types in Everything Is Crab : Predator, Prey, Gregarious, Imposing, and Trickster.

While players will end up picking evolutions associated with more than one affinity, it is often best to choose evolutions that go hand in hand with the affinities currently benefiting their strategy. For example, if they've chosen the Herbivore or Vegan evolution, picking Predator affinities won't have as much effect, as they won't get as much from meat as they do from fruits.

After leveling up a few times, players might want to pick what type of creature they want to be so they can focus on which affinities they will invest in.

Beware of Your Biome Before a Boss Fight Begins

Players will find it much more difficult to successfully defeat a boss if they're fighting in terrain or weather that is not ideal for their current evolution choices.

Subscribe to our newsletter for Everything Is Crab tips

Join our newsletter to get focused guides and practical strategies for Everything Is Crab — concise breakdowns of POIs, Mutagen point usage, affinity choices, and biome tactics so you can make smarter evolution decisions during your runs.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

To have an easier time fighting a boss, players will want to move to the grassy biome, as it has moderate weather and the terrain that is ideal for any creature. This way, they can focus on fighting the boss rather than having to find shade or constantly waiting for their dash or sprint cooldown to end.

Everything is Crab

item.title: Every Stretch Goal for Temtem: Pioneers Explained
item.description:
After a breakout launch with the original Temtem , followed by Temtem: Showdown and Temtem: Swarm , developer Crema has brought yet another project to Kickstarter within its IP, and it's called Temtem: Pioneers . Much like Temtem: Swarm chose to defy the principle of monster-collecting games with bullet hell gameplay mechanics, Temtem: Pioneers is a survival game at its core. Survival mechanics are not necessarily limiting, according to Crema, and the game very much retains that aspect of finding, taming, and training monsters to have at your side during your adventures. The monster-collecting identity of the game takes interesting turns here, as you will be able to use Tems as mounts, to battle wild Tems and Downbelow bosses, and to help collect resources.

There is a lot to unpack when it comes to Temtem: Pioneers ' Downbelow, its critters, its gameplay, and what the developer has promised so far. Interestingly, while the Kickstarter campaign has wrapped, Crema decided to allow late pledges for those who missed out on the campaign but still want to grab the game or other addons. Furthermore, the game came with seven stretch goals on Kickstarter, and they have all been cleared. They range from additional biomes all the way to game-changing features, like a PvP arena, and here's everything you should know.

TemTem: Pioneers' Confirmed Kickstarter Stretch Goals

Temtem: Pioneers ' new Tems are, of course, one of the biggest parts of the game because they build on the IP's identity and make up for the removal of Temtem 's Digital Tems.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

However, most of the stretch goals from Kickstarter focus on adding meaningful features to the game:

  • Funding goal — 90,000 euros (around $105,000): This was the goal Crema set to get development underway, and it was reached in eight hours.
  • Crystal Biome (Stretch goal 1) — 140,000 euros (around $165,000): The first stretch goal adds a new biome to the game's selection, which is the Crystal Biome. Specific encounters have not been disclosed so far, but they will most likely include a variety of Crystal-type Temtem.
  • Fossil Tems (Stretch goal 2) — 250,000 euros (around $294,000): This stretch goal adds a total of six new Temtem to Temtem: Pioneers , with three base fossil forms and one evolution each. It's unclear what this will translate into yet, but Crema confirmed that it will be a quest chain that also unlocks a dungeon.
  • Improved Mount System (Stretch goal 3) — 300,000 euros (around $352,000): The third stretch goal for Temtem: Pioneers on Kickstarter adds new features to the mount system. This allows players to use mixed-terrain mounts and go faster on each terrain by training their mounts.
  • Dark Forest Biome (Stretch goal 4) — 350,000 euros (around $411,000): This stretch goal adds the second and final extra biome to the game, this time focused on a location for Mental-type Temtem. Like the first, it's unclear exactly what it will contain, but early pictures show several returning Tems.
  • New Temtem Variations (Stretch goal 5) — 400,000 euros (around $470,000): The fifth stretch goal adds not one but two new color variations for each Temtem. If you're familiar with shiny Pokemon, each Tem previously had a Luma color palette unique to that species, with Umbra being added as a universal variation. Now, Pioneers adds Auri and Iris variations, presumably always yellow/golden Tems for the former and iridescent for the latter.
  • Customizable Game Modes (Stretch goal 6) — 450,000 euros (around $529,000): This stretch goal will allow players to customize their servers and how their survival game plays overall, both for solo play and co-op.
  • PvP Playground (Stretch goal 7) — 500,000 euros (around $588,000): The final stretch goal was reached less than 24 hours from the conclusion of the campaign, and it will offer a PvP arena for players to test their Tems against those of other players in a mode similar to battle simulation in Pokemon Showdown .

These seven are all the stretch goals for the game, and it's worth noting that no additional ones were added, and no unmet goals exist. New features that haven't been mentioned or expanded upon in the campaign may be added to the final version of the game or its alpha version for fans to test. For example, the various bosses have been mainly hinted at, with one of them confirmed as Gharunder. Crema has also confirmed that breeding is in the game and that it will be much more accessible than the original game, though it remains to be seen how.

Subscribe to the newsletter for Temtem: Pioneers insights

Looking for deeper analysis of Temtem: Pioneers? Subscribe to the newsletter for detailed coverage of stretch goals, new Tem variations, mount and survival systems, and what these changes mean for the IP and its fans.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Still, Temtem: Pioneers is shaping up to be a nice addition to Pokemon -like games and its own IP. While very different from Temtem , with real-time combat and survival mechanics, it could be the refreshing and yet familiar experience the series needs.

Follow
Followed
Temtem: Pioneers
Adventure
Survival
Open-World
Systems
Developer(s)
Crema
Publisher(s)
Crema
Multiplayer
Online Co-Op, Online Multiplayer
Franchise
Temtem

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Adventure, Survival, Open-World
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
After a breakout launch with the original Temtem , followed by Temtem: Showdown and Temtem: Swarm , developer Crema has brought yet another project to Kickstarter within its IP, and it's called Temtem: Pioneers . Much like Temtem: Swarm chose to defy the principle of monster-collecting games with bullet hell gameplay mechanics, Temtem: Pioneers is a survival game at its core. Survival mechanics are not necessarily limiting, according to Crema, and the game very much retains that aspect of finding, taming, and training monsters to have at your side during your adventures. The monster-collecting identity of the game takes interesting turns here, as you will be able to use Tems as mounts, to battle wild Tems and Downbelow bosses, and to help collect resources.

There is a lot to unpack when it comes to Temtem: Pioneers ' Downbelow, its critters, its gameplay, and what the developer has promised so far. Interestingly, while the Kickstarter campaign has wrapped, Crema decided to allow late pledges for those who missed out on the campaign but still want to grab the game or other addons. Furthermore, the game came with seven stretch goals on Kickstarter, and they have all been cleared. They range from additional biomes all the way to game-changing features, like a PvP arena, and here's everything you should know.

TemTem: Pioneers' Confirmed Kickstarter Stretch Goals

Temtem: Pioneers ' new Tems are, of course, one of the biggest parts of the game because they build on the IP's identity and make up for the removal of Temtem 's Digital Tems.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

However, most of the stretch goals from Kickstarter focus on adding meaningful features to the game:

  • Funding goal — 90,000 euros (around $105,000): This was the goal Crema set to get development underway, and it was reached in eight hours.
  • Crystal Biome (Stretch goal 1) — 140,000 euros (around $165,000): The first stretch goal adds a new biome to the game's selection, which is the Crystal Biome. Specific encounters have not been disclosed so far, but they will most likely include a variety of Crystal-type Temtem.
  • Fossil Tems (Stretch goal 2) — 250,000 euros (around $294,000): This stretch goal adds a total of six new Temtem to Temtem: Pioneers , with three base fossil forms and one evolution each. It's unclear what this will translate into yet, but Crema confirmed that it will be a quest chain that also unlocks a dungeon.
  • Improved Mount System (Stretch goal 3) — 300,000 euros (around $352,000): The third stretch goal for Temtem: Pioneers on Kickstarter adds new features to the mount system. This allows players to use mixed-terrain mounts and go faster on each terrain by training their mounts.
  • Dark Forest Biome (Stretch goal 4) — 350,000 euros (around $411,000): This stretch goal adds the second and final extra biome to the game, this time focused on a location for Mental-type Temtem. Like the first, it's unclear exactly what it will contain, but early pictures show several returning Tems.
  • New Temtem Variations (Stretch goal 5) — 400,000 euros (around $470,000): The fifth stretch goal adds not one but two new color variations for each Temtem. If you're familiar with shiny Pokemon, each Tem previously had a Luma color palette unique to that species, with Umbra being added as a universal variation. Now, Pioneers adds Auri and Iris variations, presumably always yellow/golden Tems for the former and iridescent for the latter.
  • Customizable Game Modes (Stretch goal 6) — 450,000 euros (around $529,000): This stretch goal will allow players to customize their servers and how their survival game plays overall, both for solo play and co-op.
  • PvP Playground (Stretch goal 7) — 500,000 euros (around $588,000): The final stretch goal was reached less than 24 hours from the conclusion of the campaign, and it will offer a PvP arena for players to test their Tems against those of other players in a mode similar to battle simulation in Pokemon Showdown .

These seven are all the stretch goals for the game, and it's worth noting that no additional ones were added, and no unmet goals exist. New features that haven't been mentioned or expanded upon in the campaign may be added to the final version of the game or its alpha version for fans to test. For example, the various bosses have been mainly hinted at, with one of them confirmed as Gharunder. Crema has also confirmed that breeding is in the game and that it will be much more accessible than the original game, though it remains to be seen how.

Subscribe to the newsletter for Temtem: Pioneers insights

Looking for deeper analysis of Temtem: Pioneers? Subscribe to the newsletter for detailed coverage of stretch goals, new Tem variations, mount and survival systems, and what these changes mean for the IP and its fans.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Still, Temtem: Pioneers is shaping up to be a nice addition to Pokemon -like games and its own IP. While very different from Temtem , with real-time combat and survival mechanics, it could be the refreshing and yet familiar experience the series needs.

Platform(s)

item.title: Baldur’s Gate 3: 23 Best Clothing Mods
item.description:
The joys of an RPG like Baldur’s Gate 3 do not just come from the thrilling adventures found within the fantasy world, but in the creation of one’s character. Baldur’s Gate 3 has an amazing array of outfits and clothing that will have players spending hours crafting their perfect character and party; however, if they want more options than provided by the game, they can find a plethora of mods from the Baldur’s Gate 3 community to help spice up their fashion.

These mods range from obscure armor, fan creations, new dyes, and even armor sets from other games entirely. The modding community constantly impresses when it does its best to show its passion for the game and the devs behind it, and Baldur’s Gate 3 is certainly no exception.

Updated May 10, 2026, by Mark Sammut: We have added three more BG3 mods: Zani Casual Outfit, Origin-Themed Cloaks, and Evil Gear for Good Players .

Clair Obscur At Home

When Two RPG Masterpieces Collide

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: aruless

What are mods but the ultimate love letter to a game? Inspired by other projects that they love, plenty of creators use their abilities to marry two IPs that will otherwise never come together. Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 are probably the most universally beloved RPGs of the 2020s, and they both tell great stories, have top-notch characters, and are closer to AA rather than AAA releases. Despite falling within the same genre, they are very different games, and there is no guarantee that a fan of BG3 will gravitate towards Clair Obscur . However, this modder clearly did.

This pack adds a few different armor sets from Clair Obscur : Gustave's suit, Maelle's standard uniform, Maele's pure white uniform, and Alicia's outfit. They all come with camp versions, making them all-around usable. These clothes are based on robes, so the stats will match those. These outfits are pretty great looking in a vacuum, but this mod is mainly for people who love both games.

Origin-Themed Cloaks

A Cloak For Each BG3 Companion

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Some BG3 clothing mods add attire that feels ripped right out of another universe, and they are great. However, there is a lot to be said about mods that emphasize authenticity and immersion, with the goal being to add something that makes sense for the world and characters. This mod serves as a prime example, as it adds cloaks that fit the companions' backgrounds and personalities. Consequently, they might not be the most instantly eye-catching clothes, but they just look right when they are worn by the characters.

As standard, each cloak comes with some special features. For instance, Shadowheart's Shadowed Cloak grants a bonus to spell attacks while obscured and radiant damage resistance. The Mystral Clock lets Gale cast one free spell per rest, along with a bonus for saving throws against spells. Just solid additions that make these clothes feel worthwhile rather than just set dressing.

Erudite Robe — A Potent Robe for Wizards

Now, Also For Intelligence Casters

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

While every class comes with its share of armor options, a few types need to wait quite a while to get visually interesting outfits that help them shine. Wizards have to wait longer than most before they can start to show off their taste in fashion, particularly if they want robes. The Potent Robe is available in the base game, and it uses the character's Charisma to increase their cantrips and temporary hit points. The clothing looks fantastic, but it is only really viable if you have a Charisma caster.

Using this robe as its base, this mod swaps Charisma for Intelligence, making it a viable option for wizards like Gale. Besides the shift in ability and a different default design, the Erudite Robe offers the same benefits. If you prefer the look of the Potent Robe, you can also download a version of this mod that copies over that design.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Gothyanki Gear

A Complete Package

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jerinski

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

The Githyanki are one of the many interesting aspects of Baldur's Gate 3 , and they definitely deserve the attention that the game provides them. However, they are rather limited in their cosmetics, and it's mainly just their silver armor that's present. With the Gothyanki Gear, players can add a bit more style to this race.

The Gothyanki Gear provides new leathers and themes, from cosmetics for brawlers, warlocks, pirates, and more. Plenty of cosmetic choices for the Githyanki certainly fit their character, and the outfits themselves seem very on-brand.

Wisdom Caster Armor

Balanced Armor For Clerics And Druids

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Compared to most other classes, druids and clerics seemed to get the short end of the stick when it comes to armor. If you are a magic user, you mostly have to rely on robes, which are relatively scarce and don't always provide great defense. This mod specifically focuses on these two classes and introduces new armor in all three of Baldur's Gate 3 's acts, so players always have balanced options that meet their present needs.

You can buy these armors from Brem (Act 1), Lan Tarv (Act 2), and Mystic Carrion (Act 3), and they provide a fairly wide range of benefits. The goal of this mod is not to overpower players but rather to provide an experience that fits the current difficulty level, and it works very well for that.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Airy: Tona Inspired

A Throwback To An Oblivion Mod

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: aruless

Mods inspiring newer mods is just the circle of life. Tona is a The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion modder who created detailed armor and clothes back in the late 2000s and early 2010s. Aruless used this inspiration to create a mod for Skyrim that ported some of Tona's wardrobe, and this 2026 mod does the same for Baldur's Gate 3 .

In fact, this one goes a bit further, as it is a recreation of Tona's Airy outfit, giving it a unique flair that fits BG3 . This mod comes with two variants of the outfit, with one having a plate chest that gives it a more practical aesthetic. At the moment, only human characters with the female slender body type can use it, along with elves, half-elves, drows, and tieflings.

Witcher Outfits - Dettlaff

Become The Witcher

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: Cerberry

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

This mod provides a new armor set for body types 1–4, complete with their own set of perks and traits that make them worth using. Luckily for those who still want a vanilla playthrough, these outfits won't grant outrageous bonuses, but ones that fit the theme of a Witcher rather well.

Complete with a black color scheme and thick leather, these Witcher outfits are perfect for those who want to wear the garb of a hunter. It's the perfect set for those seeking Auntie Ethel to cure her of life itself, but this outfit certainly would be cooler if it were themed around The Witcher , rather than just Witchers.

Backless Outfits Clipping Fix

Visual Glitches Be Gone

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: celadoneiron

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

With so many body types and outfits, it's no surprise that not all of them fit how they are supposed to. Luckily, if players are still having issues with clothes clipping to reveal something rather inappropriate and NSFW, then they can download the Backless Outfits Clipping Fix.

Whilst this mod won't add any new outfits, it might help the players out there who can't seem to walk or enter sneak without exposing their lower regions. It will certainly help with immersion, as players can run around freely without worrying about genital jumpscare.

Camp Robes

Use The Camp Clothing To Decide How Your Characters Look

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Camp Clothing dictates the way a character dresses at campsites, but it can also be used to visually alter their appearance while traveling. Basically, players can make characters always appear in their camp attire, while still keeping the benefits of the armor set they are actually wearing. Although the base game has quite an impressive range of Camp Clothes, they are all "casual" garments that are separate from armor or robes. You cannot pick one of the latter as Camp Clothing, meaning you cannot use them as your default visual attire.

This mod adds a plethora of simple robes as Camp Clothing, making it possible for players to constantly dress how they want without having to worry about stats or benefits. These robes are all visual and do not come with any states, as they are classified as Camp Clothes.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Tasha's Cauldron Of Outfits

Type 1 Variety

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: tashaweeds

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

New armors are gifted to players with body type 1 thanks to this mod by TashaWeeds. The detail and design of these outfits make for a beautiful, yet intimidating appearance, providing players with an assortment of designs to make them fit into the world as rulers, killers, and everything in between.

Players can only use these armors on body type 1, which is somewhat of a disadvantage of the mod, but that doesn't discredit the detail of these costumes. An assortment of gothic-themed outfits that Shadowheart is sure to love will certainly aid the fashion sense of Tav and company.
item.content:encoded:
The joys of an RPG like Baldur’s Gate 3 do not just come from the thrilling adventures found within the fantasy world, but in the creation of one’s character. Baldur’s Gate 3 has an amazing array of outfits and clothing that will have players spending hours crafting their perfect character and party; however, if they want more options than provided by the game, they can find a plethora of mods from the Baldur’s Gate 3 community to help spice up their fashion.

These mods range from obscure armor, fan creations, new dyes, and even armor sets from other games entirely. The modding community constantly impresses when it does its best to show its passion for the game and the devs behind it, and Baldur’s Gate 3 is certainly no exception.

Updated May 10, 2026, by Mark Sammut: We have added three more BG3 mods: Zani Casual Outfit, Origin-Themed Cloaks, and Evil Gear for Good Players .

Clair Obscur At Home

When Two RPG Masterpieces Collide

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: aruless

What are mods but the ultimate love letter to a game? Inspired by other projects that they love, plenty of creators use their abilities to marry two IPs that will otherwise never come together. Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 are probably the most universally beloved RPGs of the 2020s, and they both tell great stories, have top-notch characters, and are closer to AA rather than AAA releases. Despite falling within the same genre, they are very different games, and there is no guarantee that a fan of BG3 will gravitate towards Clair Obscur . However, this modder clearly did.

This pack adds a few different armor sets from Clair Obscur : Gustave's suit, Maelle's standard uniform, Maele's pure white uniform, and Alicia's outfit. They all come with camp versions, making them all-around usable. These clothes are based on robes, so the stats will match those. These outfits are pretty great looking in a vacuum, but this mod is mainly for people who love both games.

Origin-Themed Cloaks

A Cloak For Each BG3 Companion

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Some BG3 clothing mods add attire that feels ripped right out of another universe, and they are great. However, there is a lot to be said about mods that emphasize authenticity and immersion, with the goal being to add something that makes sense for the world and characters. This mod serves as a prime example, as it adds cloaks that fit the companions' backgrounds and personalities. Consequently, they might not be the most instantly eye-catching clothes, but they just look right when they are worn by the characters.

As standard, each cloak comes with some special features. For instance, Shadowheart's Shadowed Cloak grants a bonus to spell attacks while obscured and radiant damage resistance. The Mystral Clock lets Gale cast one free spell per rest, along with a bonus for saving throws against spells. Just solid additions that make these clothes feel worthwhile rather than just set dressing.

Erudite Robe — A Potent Robe for Wizards

Now, Also For Intelligence Casters

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

While every class comes with its share of armor options, a few types need to wait quite a while to get visually interesting outfits that help them shine. Wizards have to wait longer than most before they can start to show off their taste in fashion, particularly if they want robes. The Potent Robe is available in the base game, and it uses the character's Charisma to increase their cantrips and temporary hit points. The clothing looks fantastic, but it is only really viable if you have a Charisma caster.

Using this robe as its base, this mod swaps Charisma for Intelligence, making it a viable option for wizards like Gale. Besides the shift in ability and a different default design, the Erudite Robe offers the same benefits. If you prefer the look of the Potent Robe, you can also download a version of this mod that copies over that design.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Gothyanki Gear

A Complete Package

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jerinski

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

The Githyanki are one of the many interesting aspects of Baldur's Gate 3 , and they definitely deserve the attention that the game provides them. However, they are rather limited in their cosmetics, and it's mainly just their silver armor that's present. With the Gothyanki Gear, players can add a bit more style to this race.

The Gothyanki Gear provides new leathers and themes, from cosmetics for brawlers, warlocks, pirates, and more. Plenty of cosmetic choices for the Githyanki certainly fit their character, and the outfits themselves seem very on-brand.

Wisdom Caster Armor

Balanced Armor For Clerics And Druids

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Compared to most other classes, druids and clerics seemed to get the short end of the stick when it comes to armor. If you are a magic user, you mostly have to rely on robes, which are relatively scarce and don't always provide great defense. This mod specifically focuses on these two classes and introduces new armor in all three of Baldur's Gate 3 's acts, so players always have balanced options that meet their present needs.

You can buy these armors from Brem (Act 1), Lan Tarv (Act 2), and Mystic Carrion (Act 3), and they provide a fairly wide range of benefits. The goal of this mod is not to overpower players but rather to provide an experience that fits the current difficulty level, and it works very well for that.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Airy: Tona Inspired

A Throwback To An Oblivion Mod

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: aruless

Mods inspiring newer mods is just the circle of life. Tona is a The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion modder who created detailed armor and clothes back in the late 2000s and early 2010s. Aruless used this inspiration to create a mod for Skyrim that ported some of Tona's wardrobe, and this 2026 mod does the same for Baldur's Gate 3 .

In fact, this one goes a bit further, as it is a recreation of Tona's Airy outfit, giving it a unique flair that fits BG3 . This mod comes with two variants of the outfit, with one having a plate chest that gives it a more practical aesthetic. At the moment, only human characters with the female slender body type can use it, along with elves, half-elves, drows, and tieflings.

Witcher Outfits - Dettlaff

Become The Witcher

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: Cerberry

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

This mod provides a new armor set for body types 1–4, complete with their own set of perks and traits that make them worth using. Luckily for those who still want a vanilla playthrough, these outfits won't grant outrageous bonuses, but ones that fit the theme of a Witcher rather well.

Complete with a black color scheme and thick leather, these Witcher outfits are perfect for those who want to wear the garb of a hunter. It's the perfect set for those seeking Auntie Ethel to cure her of life itself, but this outfit certainly would be cooler if it were themed around The Witcher , rather than just Witchers.

Backless Outfits Clipping Fix

Visual Glitches Be Gone

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: celadoneiron

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

With so many body types and outfits, it's no surprise that not all of them fit how they are supposed to. Luckily, if players are still having issues with clothes clipping to reveal something rather inappropriate and NSFW, then they can download the Backless Outfits Clipping Fix.

Whilst this mod won't add any new outfits, it might help the players out there who can't seem to walk or enter sneak without exposing their lower regions. It will certainly help with immersion, as players can run around freely without worrying about genital jumpscare.

Camp Robes

Use The Camp Clothing To Decide How Your Characters Look

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: jonhinkerton

Camp Clothing dictates the way a character dresses at campsites, but it can also be used to visually alter their appearance while traveling. Basically, players can make characters always appear in their camp attire, while still keeping the benefits of the armor set they are actually wearing. Although the base game has quite an impressive range of Camp Clothes, they are all "casual" garments that are separate from armor or robes. You cannot pick one of the latter as Camp Clothing, meaning you cannot use them as your default visual attire.

This mod adds a plethora of simple robes as Camp Clothing, making it possible for players to constantly dress how they want without having to worry about stats or benefits. These robes are all visual and do not come with any states, as they are classified as Camp Clothes.

This mod came out before the official mod support was added to Baldur's Gate 3 , and it doesn't use that format.

Tasha's Cauldron Of Outfits

Type 1 Variety

  • Mod Link
  • Created by: tashaweeds

For Additional Mods, Visit: GGMods.com

New armors are gifted to players with body type 1 thanks to this mod by TashaWeeds. The detail and design of these outfits make for a beautiful, yet intimidating appearance, providing players with an assortment of designs to make them fit into the world as rulers, killers, and everything in between.

Players can only use these armors on body type 1, which is somewhat of a disadvantage of the mod, but that doesn't discredit the detail of these costumes. An assortment of gothic-themed outfits that Shadowheart is sure to love will certainly aid the fashion sense of Tav and company.

item.title: LEGO's Largest Set Ever May Be Accompanied by a Perfect Gift This Summer
item.description:
A LEGO set designed as a tribute to Leonardo da Vinci is expected to launch in June 2026, according to a new rumor from a reliable source. The leaked model, said to be planned as a Gift with Purchase offer, could serve as a perfect complement to LEGO's largest set ever, which is also expected to reach store shelves around the same time.

A mid-March 2026 insider report recently suggested that LEGO is working on a massive replica of Sagrada Família, the world-famous Barcelona basilica. The model is rumored to include 12,060 pieces, 365 more than LEGO's current largest set, 31203 World Map, which was released in mid-2021 and retired by the end of 2023. Sagrada Família is therefore expected to significantly expand LEGO's flagship lineup catering to adult builders interested in culture, history, or both. However, it may not be the only such set to debut in summer 2026.

Unannounced Leonardo da Vinci Set Detailed in New LEGO Leak

According to a premature listing that Instagram user and serial LEGO leaker Carter_Bricks04 dug up from the website of German retailer Brickmerge, LEGO's near-future plans include a limited-time Leonardo da Vinci tribute set. The unannounced model is said to include 251 pieces and carry an 18+ age rating. Its unofficial product page lists it under set number 40902, suggesting it is intended as one of LEGO's Gift with Purchase incentives rather than a standalone product, a theory Carter_Bricks04 supports. Although Brickmerge lists a June 1 release, some LEGO sleuths believe the set has the hallmarks of a mid-month launch and will potentially arrive closer to June 15.

LEGO Leonardo da Vinci Set Rumor Roundup

  • Name : Tribute to Leonardo da Vinci
  • No .: 40902
  • Pieces : 251
  • Age rating : 18+
  • Price : N/A | Gift with Purchase
  • Release date : mid-June 2026

Although a LEGO tribute to Leonardo da Vinci would not be a direct thematic match for Sagrada Família, the rumored promo set could still function as a fitting companion piece. Both models are said to sit within LEGO's adult-facing culture-and-history lane, which has increasingly treated the company's plastic bricks as a medium for display-first recreations of real-world art, architecture, and design milestones. Sagrada Família would represent that idea at its most ambitious, translating Antoni Gaudí's unfinished basilica into a record-breaking LEGO Architecture set. A da Vinci-themed Gift with Purchase would approach a similar audience from the opposite direction, offering a much smaller tribute to one of history's most famous artists, engineers, and inventors.

LEGO Has Technically Already Released One Leonardo da Vinci Tribute

The rumored Tribute to Leonardo da Vinci would not be the Danish toymaker's first set paying homage to the Renaissance polymath. That distinction belongs to set 10363, Leonardo da Vinci's Flying Machine, a $49.99, 493-piece model released in January 2025 and still available for purchase as of May 2026. While 10363 launched as part of the LEGO Icons theme, the 40902-designated tribute gift is expected to debut outside any of the company's official product lines.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS
—
More Games

Answers

Title

—

—

Clues

—

—

Guess the games from the emojis.
Easy (120s)Medium (90s)Hard (60s)

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.
Time Left: 00:00
Title
—
Clues
—
Results
0 POINTS
—
More Games
Answers
Title
—
—
Clues
—
—

Depending on its availability window, the 251-piece da Vinci set could plausibly overlap with Sagrada Família's rumored July 1 launch. In that event, early buyers of the landmark model could qualify for a bonus that feels more like a natural extension of their purchase than a random freebie. Sagrada Família is rumored to arrive around the same time as another massive LEGO set based on The Lord of the Rings ' Minas Tirith, which is expected to include 8,278 pieces.

Get the newsletter for LEGO leaks, deep dives, and context

Subscribe to the newsletter for expanded coverage of LEGO leaks, detailed set analysis, and context on culture-and-history adult builds. Get deeper insight into rumored releases—like Leonardo da Vinci tributes and landmark builds—and see how they fit the collector scene.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Source: ItsJustAJoe / Reddit
item.content:encoded:
A LEGO set designed as a tribute to Leonardo da Vinci is expected to launch in June 2026, according to a new rumor from a reliable source. The leaked model, said to be planned as a Gift with Purchase offer, could serve as a perfect complement to LEGO's largest set ever, which is also expected to reach store shelves around the same time.

A mid-March 2026 insider report recently suggested that LEGO is working on a massive replica of Sagrada Família, the world-famous Barcelona basilica. The model is rumored to include 12,060 pieces, 365 more than LEGO's current largest set, 31203 World Map, which was released in mid-2021 and retired by the end of 2023. Sagrada Família is therefore expected to significantly expand LEGO's flagship lineup catering to adult builders interested in culture, history, or both. However, it may not be the only such set to debut in summer 2026.

Unannounced Leonardo da Vinci Set Detailed in New LEGO Leak

According to a premature listing that Instagram user and serial LEGO leaker Carter_Bricks04 dug up from the website of German retailer Brickmerge, LEGO's near-future plans include a limited-time Leonardo da Vinci tribute set. The unannounced model is said to include 251 pieces and carry an 18+ age rating. Its unofficial product page lists it under set number 40902, suggesting it is intended as one of LEGO's Gift with Purchase incentives rather than a standalone product, a theory Carter_Bricks04 supports. Although Brickmerge lists a June 1 release, some LEGO sleuths believe the set has the hallmarks of a mid-month launch and will potentially arrive closer to June 15.

LEGO Leonardo da Vinci Set Rumor Roundup

  • Name : Tribute to Leonardo da Vinci
  • No .: 40902
  • Pieces : 251
  • Age rating : 18+
  • Price : N/A | Gift with Purchase
  • Release date : mid-June 2026

Although a LEGO tribute to Leonardo da Vinci would not be a direct thematic match for Sagrada Família, the rumored promo set could still function as a fitting companion piece. Both models are said to sit within LEGO's adult-facing culture-and-history lane, which has increasingly treated the company's plastic bricks as a medium for display-first recreations of real-world art, architecture, and design milestones. Sagrada Família would represent that idea at its most ambitious, translating Antoni Gaudí's unfinished basilica into a record-breaking LEGO Architecture set. A da Vinci-themed Gift with Purchase would approach a similar audience from the opposite direction, offering a much smaller tribute to one of history's most famous artists, engineers, and inventors.

LEGO Has Technically Already Released One Leonardo da Vinci Tribute

The rumored Tribute to Leonardo da Vinci would not be the Danish toymaker's first set paying homage to the Renaissance polymath. That distinction belongs to set 10363, Leonardo da Vinci's Flying Machine, a $49.99, 493-piece model released in January 2025 and still available for purchase as of May 2026. While 10363 launched as part of the LEGO Icons theme, the 40902-designated tribute gift is expected to debut outside any of the company's official product lines.

Sagrada Família is rumored to arrive around the same time as another massive LEGO set based on The Lord of the Rings ' Minas Tirith, which is expected to include 8,278 pieces.

Get the newsletter for LEGO leaks, deep dives, and context

Subscribe to the newsletter for expanded coverage of LEGO leaks, detailed set analysis, and context on culture-and-history adult builds. Get deeper insight into rumored releases—like Leonardo da Vinci tributes and landmark builds—and see how they fit the collector scene.

item.title: Double Fine Employees Are Planning to Unionize
item.description:
Microsoft subsidiary studio Double Fine is unionizing, according to a recent petition filed with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB). After announcing the petition, which was filed on Thursday, May 7, Double Fine stated that it was working alongside the Communication Workers of America (CWA) to secure union representation.

Double Fine was founded in July 2000 by game designer Tim Schafer following his departure from LucasArts. Since its creation, Double Fine has produced various iconic and eclectic games for players to enjoy, including Psychonauts , Brutal Legend , and Broken Age . Double Fine was acquired by Microsoft in 2019 in a standalone purchase. Since joining the Xbox umbrella, Double Fine has released additional titles for multiple platforms, including Psychonauts 2 and Keeper .

Double Fine Announces Union Election Petition

Following the launch of its latest game, a multiplayer brawler title called Kiln , Double Fine revealed that it was taking a major step to help ensure its future amid a troubling time for the gaming industry. As first reported by Aftermath, Double Fine stated that 42 part-time and full-time members would be included in its new union that it is forming in cooperation with the CWA. The NLRB petition was filed with the NLRB's regional chapter in San Francisco, where Double Fine is also located. Double Fine stated that it is seeking a labor union election alongside a voluntary recognition request from Microsoft. In a statement following the petition, the CWA said, "On May 7, the workers at Microsoft studio Double Fine Productions announced their decision to form a union with CWA to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life."

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

The case is still open as of this writing. Should its efforts prove successful, Double Fine would join other companies under Microsoft's umbrella that have successfully unionized. Further, Double Fine would also be the first union to form under new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma, who took over following the retirement of Phil Spencer in February 2026. Sharma has since added new leaders to help manage the Xbox brand, four of whom she previously worked with during her time in Microsoft's CoreAI team. Sharma also announced that development of Copilot on Xbox consoles and mobile devices would end, as they do not align with the direction Xbox is headed.

Microsoft's History With Unions May Favor Double Fine

Following Microsoft's buyouts of Zenimax and Activision Blizzard, several development teams from studios like Raven Software have worked with the CWA to form their own labor unions. In August 2025, the Diablo development team voted to form a wall-to-wall union comprised of all its members, with over 450 employees at Blizzard's Team 3 counted under the agreement at the time. The Diablo developers' union followed the successful formation of the World of Warcraft Gamemaker's Guild in 2024. The World of Warcraft union encompassed over 500 employees across its California and Massachusetts offices.

Balance the critic averages

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.

Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —

Results

0
—
More Games

Choose a game

Balance the critic averages
Easy (6)Medium (8)Hard (10)

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.
Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —
Results
0
—
More Games
Choose a game

Despite the expiration of Microsoft's previous labor union neutrality agreement, the CWA stated that Microsoft agreed to maintain its long-standing neutrality stance concerning Double Fine's unionization efforts. However, it remains to be seen when all parties involved will meet at the negotiating table.
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft subsidiary studio Double Fine is unionizing, according to a recent petition filed with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB). After announcing the petition, which was filed on Thursday, May 7, Double Fine stated that it was working alongside the Communication Workers of America (CWA) to secure union representation.

Double Fine was founded in July 2000 by game designer Tim Schafer following his departure from LucasArts. Since its creation, Double Fine has produced various iconic and eclectic games for players to enjoy, including Psychonauts , Brutal Legend , and Broken Age . Double Fine was acquired by Microsoft in 2019 in a standalone purchase. Since joining the Xbox umbrella, Double Fine has released additional titles for multiple platforms, including Psychonauts 2 and Keeper .

Double Fine Announces Union Election Petition

Following the launch of its latest game, a multiplayer brawler title called Kiln , Double Fine revealed that it was taking a major step to help ensure its future amid a troubling time for the gaming industry. As first reported by Aftermath, Double Fine stated that 42 part-time and full-time members would be included in its new union that it is forming in cooperation with the CWA. The NLRB petition was filed with the NLRB's regional chapter in San Francisco, where Double Fine is also located. Double Fine stated that it is seeking a labor union election alongside a voluntary recognition request from Microsoft. In a statement following the petition, the CWA said, "On May 7, the workers at Microsoft studio Double Fine Productions announced their decision to form a union with CWA to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life."

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

—

—

—

— —
More Games

The case is still open as of this writing. Should its efforts prove successful, Double Fine would join other companies under Microsoft's umbrella that have successfully unionized. Further, Double Fine would also be the first union to form under new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma, who took over following the retirement of Phil Spencer in February 2026. Sharma has since added new leaders to help manage the Xbox brand, four of whom she previously worked with during her time in Microsoft's CoreAI team. Sharma also announced that development of Copilot on Xbox consoles and mobile devices would end, as they do not align with the direction Xbox is headed.

Microsoft's History With Unions May Favor Double Fine

Following Microsoft's buyouts of Zenimax and Activision Blizzard, several development teams from studios like Raven Software have worked with the CWA to form their own labor unions. In August 2025, the Diablo development team voted to form a wall-to-wall union comprised of all its members, with over 450 employees at Blizzard's Team 3 counted under the agreement at the time. The Diablo developers' union followed the successful formation of the World of Warcraft Gamemaker's Guild in 2024. The World of Warcraft union encompassed over 500 employees across its California and Massachusetts offices.
